title: Germany pushes European military space command initiative
summary: Germany advocates for a unified European approach to military space operations, proposing the establishment of a European Space Component Command hosted by Germany. This initiative aims to enhance coordination among European nations in space defense.

title: CEO Series: Lux Aeterna’s Brian Taylor on what’s next for on-orbit servicing
summary: Lux Aeterna's CEO Brian Taylor discusses the challenges of disposable satellites and the potential of on-orbit servicing in an interview with Space Minds. The conversation highlights future advancements and solutions in satellite maintenance and sustainability.

title: The Propulsion Imperative Behind Golden Dome
summary: Golden Dome shifts missile defense focus to propulsion infrastructure within its distributed architecture. The article highlights the importance of propulsion in enabling the broader missile defense system.

title: Europe’s biggest space opportunity comes after launch
summary: Europe's space industry is focusing on sovereignty and launch capabilities, with major summits highlighting launcher and constellation discussions. The debate around national presence in space is gaining prominence.

title: Virgin Galactic returns Unity to flight to prepare for next-generation spaceplane
summary: Virgin Galactic's Unity spaceplane resumed flights to train pilots for its next-generation spacecraft. The return to service is part of preparations for future missions.

title: FAA requires mishap investigation into latest Starship launch
summary: The FAA has mandated a mishap investigation into SpaceX's latest Starship test flight, delaying any further launches until the probe is completed. This follows the agency's standard procedure after a failed test.

title: House Armed Services draft bill eliminates SDA, Space RCO as separate entities
summary: A draft bill from the House Armed Services Committee proposes eliminating the Space Development Agency (SDA) and Space Rapid Capabilities Office (Space RCO) as separate entities. The 2027 NDAA supports their realignment under a portfolio-based acquisition structure.

title: Mars to Titan: the next rallying call?
summary: The article discusses the future of human space exploration beyond Mars, questioning whether Mars should be a steppingstone to other destinations like Titan. It explores the idea of a 'Mars-to-somewhere else' strategy in space exploration.

title: European space industry warns EU Space Act could slow competitiveness
summary: European space industry representatives and legal experts warn that the EU Space Act may hinder competitiveness due to its slow, rigid, and bureaucratic nature. Concerns were raised during a panel discussion at SmallSat Europe.

title: As geopolitics reshape space, SpinLaunch sees an opening
summary: SpinLaunch's CEO highlights how geopolitics is reshaping the space industry, creating opportunities for the company. The article discusses the influence of sovereignty and geopolitical factors on space innovation.

title: Astrophotographer spends 115 hours capturing Horsehead Nebula in striking detail with backyard telescope
summary: An astrophotographer spent 115 hours capturing a highly detailed image of the Horsehead Nebula using a backyard telescope. The Horsehead Nebula, located in the constellation Orion, appears as a dark silhouette against glowing clouds.

title: Astronaut captures aurora magic from the ISS | Space photo of the day for May 28, 2026
summary: An astronaut aboard the International Space Station (ISS) captured a stunning photograph of auroras, showcasing the natural light display from space. The image was featured as the Space Photo of the Day for May 28, 2026.

title: What's next for SpaceX's Starship V3 megarocket after its historic debut flight?
summary: SpaceX's Starship V3 megarocket completed a successful debut flight, marking a significant milestone for the next-generation vehicle. The company is now preparing for future missions and potential upgrades.

title: How to catch the perfect full moonrise — just in time for the Blue Moon show on May 30
summary: A rare Blue Moon will occur on May 30, and timing is crucial to catch its full moonrise. The article provides guidance on planning to observe it precisely.

title: Most powerful 'ghost particle' ever may have come from a cosmic particle accelerator fed by a black hole
summary: The most energetic neutrino ever detected may have originated from a blazar powered by a black hole, indicating these cosmic objects act as particle accelerators. This discovery suggests black holes play a key role in accelerating high-energy particles in the universe.

title: Enjoy the Season 5 finale of 'For All Mankind' and be first to view spinoff series 'Star City' anywhere with these exclusive VPN deals — save 87% off and a bonus free $30 Amazon Gift Card
summary: A promotion for the Season 5 finale of 'For All Mankind' and its spinoff series 'Star City' offers exclusive VPN deals with Surfshark, including an 87% discount and a $30 Amazon Gift Card.

title: Where did Mercury get its water ice? Maybe from a single slow asteroid impact
summary: A new study suggests Mercury may have acquired much of its polar ice from a single slow asteroid impact within a day. The research proposes that this event occurred during a single Mercurian day following the impact.

title: Russian cosmonauts install sun-watching telescope on ISS during 6-hour spacewalk
summary: Russian cosmonauts Sergey Kud-Sverchkov and Sergei Mikaev installed a sun-watching telescope and retrieved science experiments during a 6-hour spacewalk outside the ISS on May 27, 2026.

title: China shakes up its space programs to land astronauts on the moon by 2030: 'We will spare no effort'
summary: China is integrating its robotic lunar probe missions with its human spaceflight program to achieve a crewed moon landing by 2030. The country has pledged to prioritize this goal with full effort.

title: FAA grounds SpaceX's Starship V3 megarocket after Flight 12 'mishap'
summary: The FAA has grounded SpaceX's Starship V3 megarocket after its Flight 12 ended in a mishap. An investigation is now required before the rocket can fly again.

title: Rovers and Drones laying the foundations for NASA Moon Base
summary: NASA has outlined initial missions for its Moon Base initiative, utilizing rovers and drones to establish foundational infrastructure. The initiative involves rebranding existing Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLPS) missions.

title: Following Starship V3 debut, SpaceX prepares for follow up
summary: SpaceX successfully conducted the first flight of Starship Version 3 this week. The company is now preparing for follow-up missions after the debut.

title: Launch Preview: Starlink and Amazon Leo missions fill manifest
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled this week, with four Falcon 9 Starlink missions and Amazon's Leo mission among the highlights.

title: Vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance keep Expedition 74 busy through April
summary: Expedition 74 aboard the International Space Station has been occupied with vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance activities throughout April. The crew's workload highlights ongoing operations and research on the ISS.

title: Shenzhou 23 launches new crew to Tiangong space station
summary: Shenzhou 23 successfully launched a new crew to China's Tiangong space station, which has been permanently occupied since June 2022. This mission marks another step in China's ongoing efforts to maintain and expand its presence in low Earth orbit.

title: Starship Flight 12: Block 3 Impresses on First Flight
summary: Starship Flight 12, featuring Block 3, successfully launched on May 22, 2026, from SpaceX's facilities. The mission marked a significant milestone with impressive performance during its first flight.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Electron, and Vega C launch missions alongside Starship Flight 12
summary: Six space launches are scheduled globally in the next week, including SpaceX's Starship Flight 12, a Falcon 9 mission, an Electron launch, and a Vega C flight.

title: Vega C launches SMILE to study Earth’s magnetosphere
summary: ESA's SMILE mission launched aboard Vega C to study Earth's magnetosphere. This mission aims to enhance understanding of the Sun-Earth connection.

title: Super Heavy Block 3 the Booster of the Future
summary: The article discusses the evolution of the Super Heavy Block 3 booster, the first stage of SpaceX's Starship, highlighting its advancements. It is presented as a significant development in the future of spaceflight.

title: Artemis III deep into processing and planning for key HLS test
summary: NASA is progressing with planning and processing for Artemis III, a significant crewed mission. The mission includes key Human Landing System (HLS) tests.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned twice in the provided news snippet, but no specific details or context are given about its relevance to the space industry.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. This commemorative publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration and science.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports European space science activities by involving research institutes.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive space conditions. The section is supported by young researchers contributing innovative ideas.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space activities.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test involving a parachute system, likely for a space mission, where the parachute was subjected to high temperatures to simulate re-entry conditions. This test is essential for ensuring the reliability of the parachute during actual space missions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will help slow the rover's descent during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed to improve space debris handling and satellite servicing. This innovation could enhance the safety and efficiency of in-space operations.

title: Observable Space Raises $90M Series A
summary: Observable Space, a space-tech startup, has raised $90 million in a Series A funding round to expand its laser and optical hardware production and international presence beyond the US.

title: Space Markets Emerges From Stealth With Coinbase Ventures Investment
summary: Space Markets has emerged from stealth mode with a new investment from Coinbase Ventures. The company aims to create futures markets for space-related commodities and resources.

title: Exclusive: Stellar Alpina Raises $4.5M to Build Rotating Detonation Engines
summary: Stellar Alpina, a European propulsion startup, has raised $4.5 million to develop rotating detonation engines, a novel technology aimed at advancing spacecraft propulsion. This funding will support the company's efforts to bring this innovative thrust method to the commercial market.

title: Aitech Upgrades its Space Supercomputer
summary: Aitech has upgraded its S-A2300 COTS AI Supercomputer by integrating NVIDIA’s IGX Thor platform, significantly enhancing in-orbit data processing capabilities for customers.

title: NASA Awards LTV, Lunar Transport Contracts
summary: NASA has awarded contracts for the Lunar Terrain Vehicle (LTV) and lunar transport services, marking a significant step toward returning humans to the Moon. The announcement was made during a press conference led by NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman.

title: Op-ed: Commercial Demand in LEO is Lacking. The Government Can Help.
summary: Commercial demand in Low Earth Orbit (LEO) for in-space manufacturing is insufficient to sustain itself, requiring government support to drive growth.

title: Optical Links: When to Incorporate Them? When Not To?
summary: The article discusses the cost-effectiveness of optical links for satellite applications, noting that they often cost hundreds of thousands of dollars and may not be worth the investment. It explores scenarios where optical links are beneficial and where they are not.

title: Satellogic Lands $18M+ Defense-Monitoring Contract
summary: Satellogic secured a defense-monitoring contract worth over $18 million, marking its first major agreement following a strategic pivot to persistent-monitoring capabilities.

title: SpaceX Launches Starship V3 for the First Time
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Starship V3 rocket for the first time, marking its twelfth test flight and introducing a range of upgrades. The event highlights SpaceX's continuous advancements in spaceflight technology.

title: SEOPS Will Start Waymaker LEO Rideshares In 2028
summary: SEOPS is launching Waymaker LEO Rideshares in 2028 to accommodate time-sensitive or non-standard payloads that current rideshare options cannot support.

title: Will the European Launch Market Finally Ignite?
summary: The European space industry is seeking more efficient and reliable access to space, a topic that dominated a recent launch panel discussion in Amsterdam. The question of when this will be achieved remains a key challenge for the sector.

title: Archangel Lightworks Demonstrates Deployable Optical Ground Station
summary: Archangel Lightworks successfully tested its deployable optical ground station Terra-M, achieving data transfer with a LEO satellite. The company conducted field tests to validate the technology's performance.

title: Anuvu Taps Eutelsat for IFC Capacity on Eutelsat 10B
summary: Anuvu has signed a multi-year agreement with Eutelsat to utilize satellite capacity on the Eutelsat 10B satellite for in-flight connectivity (IFC) services. The Eutelsat 10B satellite is positioned at 10 degrees East.

title: European Commission Reserves Portion of Critical MSS Spectrum for EU Operators
summary: The European Commission has outlined a process to allocate licenses for critical mobile satellite services (MSS) spectrum in Europe, prioritizing EU operators. This decision aims to regulate and manage spectrum usage within the region.

title: Space Force Awards SpaceX $2.3 Billion Contract for Space Data Network
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded SpaceX a $2.29 billion contract to develop the Space Data Network (SDN) Backbone, a proliferated Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) system. This contract aims to enhance secure and resilient data transmission capabilities for military and government operations.

title: Rideshare Providers SEOPS and Exolaunch Buy Dedicated Falcon 9 Missions
summary: SEOPS and Exolaunch have purchased dedicated SpaceX Falcon 9 missions to run their own rideshare missions. The deals were announced during Smallsat Europe.

title: Connected Car Market Picks Up Steam With Standards and NTN, Experts Say
summary: The connected car market is gaining momentum due to the establishment of standards and the adoption of Non-Terrestrial Networks (NTN), according to experts at SmallSat Europe. BMW Group's Olaf Eckart highlighted the industry's readiness to realize its potential.

title: American Airlines to Equip More Than 500 Planes with Starlink Wi-Fi
summary: American Airlines has partnered with SpaceX to equip over 500 narrowbody Airbus aircraft with Starlink's in-flight Wi-Fi service. This deal marks a significant expansion of Starlink's aviation connectivity offerings.

title: Space Force Awards Viasat and Intelsat General $438M for Continued Work on PTS-G
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded Viasat and Intelsat General a $438 million contract for satellite work under the Protected Tactical Satellite-Global (PTS-G) program. The contract aims to continue development and support for secure satellite communications.

title: ST Engineering iDirect’s New CEO Kuppanna Talks Ground Tech’s Cloud-Native, Interoperable Future
summary: ST Engineering iDirect appointed Sridhar Kuppanna as its new CEO to lead the company's cloud-native and interoperable ground technology solutions amid a dynamic market. The move comes as the satellite industry faces disruption from competitors like Starlink.
